name: The Cheshire Cat
primary_sources: Alice's Adventures in Wonderland by Lewis Carroll

distinctive_features: ability to disappear gradually leaving only the grin
visual_attributes.structural_arrangement: head and body can appear and disappear independently

domain_specific_visual.grin: iconic and remains visible even when the rest of the body disappears
domain_specific_visual.disappearance: gradual, often starting with the body and ending with the grin

functional_attributes.primary_function: to provide philosophical insights and create confusion
secondary_functions: to challenge the Queen of Hearts

domain_specific_functional.symbolic_role: represents the absurdity and unpredictability of Wonderland

relationships.description: The Cheshire Cat is associated with the Duchess in the story.
Engages Alice in conversation and philosophical debates.
Challenges the authority of the Queen of Hearts.

contextual_attributes.cultural_significance: symbolizes the whimsical and nonsensical nature of Wonderland
primary_sources: Alice's Adventures in Wonderland by Lewis Carroll
